questions.prompt: On the Oregon CCB exam blueprint, how many scored items are in the Jobsite Safety domain?
questions.explanation: The PSI Candidate Information Bulletin lists Jobsite Safety as 4 scored items in the Oregon construction contractor exam outline.

questions.prompt: Under Oregon excavation rules, when must a trench have a safe means of egress so workers do not travel more than 25 feet laterally?
choices: When the trench is 4 feet deep or more
When the trench is 3 feet deep or more
Only when work lasts more than one day
questions.explanation: Subdivision P requires a safe means of egress for trench excavations 4 feet or deeper, arranged so no worker must travel more than 25 feet laterally.

questions.prompt: Which statement best matches Oregon ladder safety requirements in construction?
choices: Defective ladders must be removed from service or tagged, and portable side rails should extend at least 3 feet above the landing unless equivalent support is provided.
Defective ladders may be used if only one rung is damaged.
Portable side rails only need to extend 1 foot above the landing.
No ladder extension requirement applies if a supervisor is present.
questions.explanation: Subdivision X requires defective ladders to be removed/tagged and generally requires portable ladder side rails to extend at least 3 feet above the upper landing unless equivalent grasping support is used.
